rationale: Sustained editorial critique framing Gucci's decline as self-inflicted strategic failure, directly damaging brand-desirability narrative in NEA.
Plain factual report of store-rationalisation plan; framed as deliberate strategy with no clear net positive or negative editorial verdict.
Hard earnings data: Gucci revenue -8% comparable, Asia-Pacific the principal drag; structural over-distribution and cultural-resonance issues cited explicitly.
Outlook piece projects only low-single-digit growth at best for Gucci in China through 2026 with consumer confidence described as fragile.
Positive third-party runway review establishing Demna's debut as a legible new identity with clear NEA-relevant hero accessories.
Quantitative demand signal: Gucci +4 positions to No. 5 on Lyst Index Q1 2026, highest demand share at Milan Fashion Week — strongest measurable brand-heat data point in the pool.

Ambassador coverage at a brand-staged Cruise 2027 show: two NEA figures drove earned media across Chinese/Korean platforms, but no quantified viral metric is given in-item — activation coverage (ambassador at brand show) with descriptive/interpretive framing only; magnitude capped at 0.5; v1.1 activation discount applied.
Positive campaign-launch coverage with dual Korea-China ambassador targeting; product traction signal present but no viral or sales data yet.
Positive ambassador profile reinforcing Gucci's China youth strategy; minor saturation-risk caveat noted but net brand-amplification effect is positive.
Positive cultural-event coverage in Korean media linking Demna's creative language to Gucci heritage ahead of planned Seoul activation.
Fandom-driven fan-billboard action amplified Cruise 2027 visibility; dampened to 0.5 per rubric fandom-spike rule — secondary earned media, not organic brand affinity.
Confirmed closure of two Shanghai flagships reinforces negative China footprint narrative; T3 standing-context item, older date, so magnitude dampened.
